label: Saltwax Back Chamber
description: Saltwax Back Chamber forms a compact sleeping room set into the brick thickness along the inner rampart wall, its corners squared by salvaged ashlar blocks. Limewashed plaster and a low-beamed ceiling confine the space, while a shuttered slit window admits a narrow bar of daylight and keeps the wards’ night-glare contained. An iron-hasped door closes to a simple peg rail, a rope-slung pallet frame, and a battered chest sized for travel papers and licensed seals. A wall plaque repeats Varric Holdfast’s interdiction symbols in plain script, treating frontier hazard codes as ordinary household instruction.
